en.extract:
The Sabah partridge (Tropicoperdix graydoni) is a bird species in the family Phasianidae. It is found in Borneo.
It was formerly considered conspecific with the chestnut-necklaced partridge (T. charltonii), but can be distinguished by the buff scaling on the belly and the lack of rufous on the head.

The '''Sabah partridge''' ('''''Tropicoperdix graydoni''''') is a [[bird]] species in the family [[Phasianidae]]. It is found in [[Borneo]].

It was formerly considered conspecific with the [[chestnut-necklaced partridge]] (''T. charltonii''), but can be distinguished by the buff scaling on the belly and the lack of rufous on the head.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Sabah Partridge - eBird |url=https://ebird.org/species/chnpar3 |access-date=2023-04-24 |website=ebird.org |language=en}}</ref>
